title: Geissoloma marginatum
intro: Geissoloma marginatum, also known as Mountain Pepper or Kanae, is a perennial shrub native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Celastraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Geissoloma marginatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used as a food ingredient to add flavor and medicinal properties to dishes, particularly in traditional Maori cuisine.
taxonomy: Geissoloma marginatum is a flowering plant species. It belongs to the family Rhamnaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as buckthorns or dogwoods. Geissoloma marginatum is native to New Zealand. It is a shrub species, found in alpine and subalpine regions of the South Island.
